title: Corybas himalaicus
intro: Corybas himalaicus is a species of orchid found in the Himalayan region, commonly known as Himalayan Corybas or Himalayan Fairy Orchid. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Corybas himalaicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Corybas himalaicus is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive problems and other health issues.
taxonomy: Corybas himalaicus is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, which includes over 30,000 species. Corybas himalaicus is further classified as a genus within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The species is a type of terrestrial orchid, which grows in the wild in various parts of the world. Within the orchid family, Corybas himalaicus is placed in the tribe Diurideae.
